admin_panel.notes: Default credentials are admin/admin as documented in the official ZTE user manual. A secondary account (user/user) has limited rights. Exetel-supplied units arrive pre-configured via Automatic Configuration Server (ACS) for Exetel's PPPoE service — the admin password may remain admin/admin but verify after a factory reset.

ethernet.pppoe_clear_note: **Switching from another nbn provider?** You may see their connection listed on this page. Delete it before continuing — you'll set up a fresh one in the next step.
steps_ipoe: Connect an Ethernet cable from the nbn connection box (NTD for FTTP, HFC connection box, or FTTC DPU) to the red WAN port on the rear of the H268A
Connect to the modem via Ethernet or Wi-Fi
Open a browser and go to http://192.168.20.1
Log in with username: admin and password: admin
Click 'Internet' in the top navigation bar
Click 'WAN' in the left sidebar, then click the 'Ethernet' tab
If an existing Exetel WAN connection is listed (e.g. Exetel_EthWAN), delete it or click 'Create New Item'
Set Connection Name to a name of your choice (e.g. Belong)
Set Type to Routing
Set Service List to INTERNET
Set MTU to 1500
Set Link Type to IP
Set IP Type to DHCP
Set IP Version to IPv4 + IPv6
Set NAT to On
Set VLAN to Off
Leave Username and Password fields blank
Click Save
Wait 1–2 minutes for the modem to connect — the Internet LED on the front should turn solid green

dsl.pppoe_clear_note: **Switching from another nbn provider?** You may see their connection listed on this page. Delete it before continuing — you'll set up a fresh one in the next step.
steps_ipoe: Connect a phone cable (RJ-11) from the nbn wall socket to the grey DSL port on the rear of the H268A
Connect to the modem via Ethernet or Wi-Fi
Open a browser and go to http://192.168.20.1
Log in with username: admin and password: admin
Click 'Internet' in the top navigation bar
Click 'WAN' in the left sidebar, then click the 'DSL' tab
If an existing Exetel WAN connection is listed (e.g. Exetel_VDSL), delete it or click 'Create New Item'
Set Connection Name to a name of your choice (e.g. Belong)
Set DSL Transfer Mode to PTM
Set Type to Routing
Set Service List to INTERNET
Set MTU to 1500
Set Link Type to IP
Set IP Type to DHCP
Set IP Version to IPv4 + IPv6
Set NAT to On
Set VLAN to Off
Leave Username and Password fields blank
Click Save
Wait 1–2 minutes for the modem to sync and connect — the DSL LED should turn solid green, followed by the Internet LED

physical.wan_port_position: on the rear panel, rightmost of the RJ-45 ports
physical.dsl_port_position: on the rear panel, leftmost position
physical.reset_button: recessed pinhole on the rear panel

factory_reset.method: With the device powered on, insert a pin or paperclip into the recessed Reset pinhole on the rear panel and hold for 10 seconds until the front LEDs change, then release. The device will restart automatically.
factory_reset.notes: Factory reset erases all custom WAN settings, Wi-Fi name and password, and any Exetel ACS-downloaded configuration. The official ZTE manual states to hold for more than 5 seconds; ISP setup guides recommend 10 seconds for a reliable reset.

firmware.check_path: Management & Diagnosis > System Management > Software Upgrade
firmware.notes: Exetel released custom firmware builds (e.g. V1.0.1 ETL.1T10) for this modem. These are Exetel-specific builds and will not be applicable when using the modem with Belong. Check the ZTE international support site (http://support.zte.com.cn) for generic firmware if needed. ISP firmware updates stop when using the modem with a different provider.

setup.setup_notes: This modem is supplied by Exetel and arrives pre-configured to automatically download Exetel's PPPoE WAN settings from Exetel's configuration server (ACS/TR-069). When switching to Belong (IPoE), you must create a new WAN connection with Link Type: IP and IP Type: DHCP, and remove or disable the existing Exetel WAN entry. Belong uses IPoE/DHCP — no username, password, or VLAN is required. The WAN UI has separate tabs for Ethernet-based (FTTP/HFC/FTTC) and DSL-based (FTTN/FTTB) connections — select the correct tab for your nbn technology type.

setup_confidence.notes: Admin IP (192.168.20.1) and credentials (admin/admin) confirmed by official ZTE user manual and multiple ISP setup guides. WAN nav path (Internet > WAN > Ethernet/DSL) confirmed by two independent ISP guides (MATE, Flipconnect). IPoE label (Link Type: IP, IP Type: DHCP) confirmed via MATE guide. Physical port colors (WAN=red, LAN=yellow, DSL=grey) confirmed via MATE guide descriptions. Firmware update path confirmed via Whirlpool search results referencing maintenance manual. Deductions: ISP-branded Exetel firmware with custom ACS pre-configuration (-15), firmware update path not confirmed by a directly fetched second source (-10), physical port positions not confirmed from manual diagram (manual is diagram-only for rear panel layout, descriptions inferred from ISP guides) (-5).
